element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns reaction timing: wait for eyes to flash, then shoot
Player must manage multiple threat sources and distribute attention
Targets appear from multiple fixed locations; spatial awareness required
Drawing gun before eyes flash disables input for remainder of duel; teaches patience discipline

typical_tension_triggers: Dual duel mode with split attention required
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Discrete duel cycles with clear tension build (waiting phase) and release (shooting phase). Player controls pace through reaction speed. Speech bubble signals imminent challenge moment. Rapid successive duels create accumulating pressure.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Single duel mode establishes baseline reaction mechanics with one opponent
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Dual duel mode introduces divided attention and simultaneous threat management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Saloon gallery mode adds spatial positioning and multiple fixed target windows
notable_spikes: Transition from single to dual opponent mode (attention splitting)
Introduction of gallery mode with distributed target locations

notable_level_patterns.description: Player faces opponent(s) in standoff. Eyes flash as signal. Player must shoot before opponent draws. Premature shooting results in automatic loss of duel (foul penalty). Next duel appears regardless of outcome.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any single or dual duel mode encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Multiple opponents appear from saloon windows as fixed spatial locations. Player shoots opponents appearing from designated windows rather than reacting to eye-flash signal.
Defeat indicated by Frédéric Chopin's Funeral March audio cue. Player loses game after failing duels (being shot first or fouling)
